chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, MIRPURKHAS Criminal Jail Appeal No.S-04 of 2024 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For order on M.A No.101/2026 2. For order on M.A No.102/2026 3. For order on M.A No.103/2026 4. For hearing of main case 09.03.2026 Ms. Nosheen Pahore, advocate for the appellant. Mr. Shah Nawaz Janjhi, advocate for the complainant. Mr. Neel Parkash, Deputy P.G. = On the last date of hearing, a report was called from the learned District & Sessions Judge, Umerkot/Trial Court for verification of the compromise filed earlier. Such report is received which states that reports were called from the Mukhtiarkar Taluka Samaro (Revenue) and the S.H.O, P.S, Samaro, who have confirmed that Mst. Sasui (deceased) died un-married, leaving behind legal heirs Muhammad Hassan (Father/complainant), Mst. Aami (Mother), Mian Bux (Brother), Muhammad Ali (Brother) and Rafique (Brother). After such verification, publication was made in the daily Kawish dated 21.02.2026; however, no one appeared to oppose the compromise. Statements of the legal heirs were recorded in open Court, wherein they stated that they have entered into a compromise with the accused in the name of Almighty Allah and have waived their rights of Qisas and Diyat. They further confirmed that they have not received any compensation in this regard. In view of the above, the compromise application already filed before this Court under Section 345 Cr.P.C is accepted and the application filed under Section 345 Cr.P.C for acquittal of the appellant/accused is allowed. Consequently, the present appellant is acquitted of all the charges as per the compromise accepted.

In view of the above, the compromise application already filed before this Court under Section 345 Cr.P.C is accepted and the application filed under Section 345 Cr.P.C for acquittal of the appellant/accused is allowed. Consequently, the present appellant is acquitted of all the charges as per the compromise accepted. He may be released forthwith if not required in any other case. JUDGE *Chander Kumar*
